records.ety: [L. eliminatus, p. p. of eliminare; e out + limen threshold; prob. akin to limes boundary. See Limit.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Eliminated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Eliminating (?).]

defs: To put out of doors; to expel; to discharge; to release; to set at liberty.
quotes: Eliminate my spirit, give it range Through provinces of thought yet unexplored.

defs: To cause to disappear from an equation; as, to eliminate an unknown quantity.
To set aside as unimportant in a process of inductive inquiry; to leave out of consideration.
quotes: Eliminate errors that have been gathering and accumulating.

defs: To obtain by separating, as from foreign matters; to deduce; as, to eliminate an idea or a conclusion.
To separate; to expel from the system; to excrete; as, the kidneys eliminate urea, the lungs carbonic acid; to eliminate poison from the system.
